content: Monounsaturated fats have one unsaturated carbon bond, making them less saturated than fully saturated fats. Examples include avocados, olive oil, nuts, and nut oils.

Omega 9s (also known as Alayich acid) help reduce blood pressure by working on adrenal centers to dilate and constrict blood vessels. They also act as antioxidants by replacing omega-3s and omega-6s in cell membranes.
A study by the Cleveland Clinic showed that 220 milligrams of Omega 7s daily reduced C reactive protein levels by 444%. Omega7s protect Omega3s from becoming toxic and damaging cells.
Polyunsaturated fats are unstable and can oxidize easily. They are important for metabolism and health. Omega-3s are anti-inflammatory and Omega-6s are pro-inflammatory.
Saturated fats are fully saturated with hydrogen molecules. They are stable and best to cook with. No saturated fats are bad they are needed for myelin sheath production.
Trans fats are made by artificially saturating unsaturated fats. They take a long time to break down and cause more body fat storage. They promote prostaglandin2 which causes more inflammation.
